source.multipart_basis: The original sample already specifies body_count=assembly for the 2-story light-wood frame in 2x4 lumber with sheathing. Multipart decomposition exposes the studs, plates, joists, rafters, headers, sheathing panels, and sole plates as distinct part families.

full_prompt:
Design the ASCE CN4 Timber Strong 2-story 2.44 m x 2.44 m x 4.88 m light wood-frame module as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the 2x4 stick-frame building as the physical collection of studs, top/bottom plates, floor joists, rafters, headers, wood structural panel sheathing for the shear walls and diaphragms, and the sill/sole plates that anchor the module to its foundation.

The assembly consists of seven part families: (A) vertical wall studs (38 mm x 89 mm nominal 2x4) at 406 mm (16 in) on-center in the four shear walls of each story, cut to story-height clear span; (B) horizontal top plates and bottom plates (double top plate per conventional framing) running along the four walls of each story; (C) second-floor joists (2x4 nominal) spanning the 2.44 m short direction at 406 mm on-center; (D) roof rafters (2x4 nominal) spanning the 2.44 m roof direction at 406 mm on-center; (E) headers (doubled 2x4 nominal) above door/window openings where specified by the team; (F) wood structural panel sheathing (OSB or plywood) on the shear walls and the horizontal floor/roof diaphragms providing lateral stiffness against the 2.22 kN racking load; (G) sill/sole plates bolted to the foundation via anchor bolts (NDS 2024 anchored-sill detail) plus the nail-schedule fasteners that connect sheathing to framing.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: module plan 2.44 x 2.44 m, story height 2.44 m, 2 stories for a total 4.88 m height, all members 2x4 nominal (38 x 89 mm).

Material inherits verbatim: Douglas Fir-Larch, Southern Pine, Hem-Fir, or SPF No.2 grade per NDS 2024 reference design values (Fb 6.2 MPa, Ft 4.0 MPa, Fc 9.3 MPa, E 11.0 GPa for DF-L No.2 bounding example).

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 floor live 1.92 kPa uniform on second floor; LC2 floor dead 0.72 kPa uniform on second floor; LC3 roof live 0.96 kPa uniform on roof; LC4 roof dead 0.72 kPa uniform on roof; LC5 concentrated 2.22 kN racking load at roof level in the weak direction.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) every stud, plate, joist, rafter, header, and shear wall must achieve NDS 2024 DCR <= 1.0 with appropriate adjustment factors (CD, CM, Ct, CL, CP, Cfu, Ci, Cr); (R2) mid-floor deflection under 1.0 live + 1.0 dead <= 6.8 mm (span/360 for 2.44 m joist span); (R3) inter-story drift under 2.22 kN racking <= 6.1 mm (H/400 for 2.44 m story); (R4) total structural lumber volume <= 0.15 m^3 as an efficiency target. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: sheathing-to-framing nail-schedule DCR and sill-plate anchor-bolt DCR must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. An NDS 2024 ASD capacity spreadsheet is required outside the FEA for member adjustment-factor checks. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (each stud, each plate, each joist, each rafter, each header, each sheathing panel, each sole plate) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..G) and instance_index, plus a cut list tagged with species and grade and a structural report listing member DCR maxima, mid-floor deflection, roof drift, total lumber volume, assembly mass, and sheathing-to-framing / sill-anchor connection DCR.
short_prompt:
Design an ASCE CN4 Timber Strong 2-story 2x4 light wood-frame module as a multi-part stick-framed assembly (wall studs, top/bottom plates, second-floor joists, roof rafters, headers, wood structural panel sheathing, sill/sole plates + nail/anchor fasteners).

Plan 2.44 x 2.44 m, story height 2.44 m, 2 stories totalling 4.88 m; 2x4 nominal 38 x 89 mm at 406 mm on-centre. DF-L / SP / HF / SPF No.2 grade per NDS 2024 with DF-L properties Fb 6.2 MPa, Ft 4.0 MPa, Fc 9.3 MPa, E 11.0 GPa.

Load cases: LC1 floor live 1.92 kPa; LC2 floor dead 0.72 kPa; LC3 roof live 0.96 kPa; LC4 roof dead 0.72 kPa; LC5 racking 2.22 kN at roof level weak direction.

Pass/fail: NDS 2024 DCR <= 1.0; mid-floor deflection <= 6.8 mm (span/360 for 2.44 m joist); roof drift <= 6.1 mm (H/400 for 2.44 m story); total lumber volume <= 0.15 m3; FEM at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: NDS 2024 member capacity with adjustment factors (CD, CM, Ct, CL, CP, Cfu, Ci, Cr) is a closed-form spreadsheet check on top of the linear FEA demands.

prompt.title: 2-story 8x8 ft light wood-frame module (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 2.44 x 2.44 x 4.88 m light wood-frame module: studs (A) + top/bottom plates (B) + second-floor joists (C) + rafters (D) + headers (E) + sheathing panels (F) + sole plates + nail-schedule/anchor fasteners (G). Verified at assembly level against NDS 2024 DCR <= 1.0, mid-floor deflection <= 6.8 mm, drift <= 6.1 mm, lumber volume <= 0.15 m^3, and nail/anchor connection DCR <= 1.0.

parts.name: wall_stud
parts.role: 2x4 (38 x 89 mm) nominal vertical stud at 406 mm (16 in) on-center in each of the four shear walls per story; carries gravity from the top plate to the bottom plate
parts.count_note: Approximately 6-8 studs per 2.44 m wall x 4 walls x 2 stories = 48-64 studs (corners and openings adjust this).

parts.name: top_bottom_plate
parts.role: horizontal 2x4 top plate (doubled) and bottom plate running along each wall per story; distributes vertical load between studs and forms the top chord of the wall diaphragm
parts.count_note: 3 plates per wall (2 top + 1 bottom) x 4 walls x 2 stories = 24 plate runs, each cut to ~2.44 m

parts.name: second_floor_joist
parts.role: 2x4 nominal floor joist spanning the 2.44 m direction at 406 mm on-center; carries the 1.92 kPa live + 0.72 kPa dead second-floor load

parts.name: roof_rafter
parts.role: 2x4 nominal rafter spanning the roof direction at 406 mm on-center; carries the 0.96 kPa live + 0.72 kPa dead roof load

parts.name: header
parts.role: doubled 2x4 nominal header above a door or window opening in a shear wall; bridges interrupted studs across the opening
parts.count_note: 0 if no openings; typical designs include 1-2 headers for access

parts.name: sheathing_panel
parts.role: wood structural panel (OSB or plywood) on shear walls and horizontal floor/roof diaphragms; provides lateral stiffness against 2.22 kN racking load
parts.count_note: approximately 4 wall panels per story x 2 stories = 8, plus floor and roof diaphragm panels

parts.name: sill_plate_and_fastener_schedule
parts.role: sole/sill plate bolted to the foundation via anchor bolts plus the nail-schedule fasteners (8d or 10d common nails) that connect sheathing to framing; carries the lateral shear transfer from the diaphragm to the foundation
parts.material_hint: sill plate same species as framing; anchor rods ASTM F1554; nails 8d/10d common

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: building coordinates: +x east wall, +y north wall, +z up from foundation

boundary_conditions.sole_plates: fixed to foundation platform via anchor bolts

objective.primary: pass all NDS 2024 DCR and deflection limits under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: NDS 2024 Chapter 12 fastener capacity for nails/bolts plus APA sheathing shear-wall tables

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for studs/plates/joists, shell for sheathing diaphragms, rigid links at nail/fastener schedule

interfaces.load_path: sheathing-to-stud nail schedule develops the shear-wall capacity against the 2.22 kN racking load
stud end nailed/toenailed into top and bottom plates; transfers gravity and uplift
floor joist top nailed into double top plate (or joist hanger); transfers floor gravity into the wall
rafter to top-plate connection; transfers roof gravity into the wall
anchor bolts through the sole plate tie the module to the foundation; transfers sliding and uplift reactions
floor diaphragm sheathing nailed to the joists; develops horizontal diaphragm shear

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Light wood-frame construction is inherently a stick-built assembly of dozens of 2x4s with sheathing; the multipart sidecar formalizes the stud / plate / joist / rafter / sheathing decomposition.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact stud count, plate-run count, and sheathing-panel count depend on door/window layout and are speculated; the original sample does not call out openings explicitly.
